title: Creamy Smoky Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring tender lamb, smoky spices, and a creamy sauce, all slow-cooked to perfection in a traditional ttukbaegi earthenware pot. This kosher-friendly recipe is perfect for a special occasion or a cozy night in.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the ttukbaegi earthenware pot over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic cloves, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, heavy cream, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Stir in the chopped parsley and serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Traditionally, the tagine is served with crusty bread or over couscous, but it can also be served with rice or roasted vegetables.
Leftovers can be refrigerated for up to 3 days or frozen for up to 2 months.
